---
title: PubChem
alias: &ALIAS pubchem
href: https://pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

PubChem (NCBI) is the world's largest freely accessible chemical database, providing information on chemical structures, identifiers, properties, bioactivities, and biomedical literature.

## Key Features

- Contains over 100 million compounds with 2D/3D structures, InChI keys, and SMILES representations
- Aggregates bioactivity data from high-throughput screening assays across thousands of targets
- Links chemical records to PubMed literature, gene targets, pathways, and clinical trials
- Provides PUG REST and PUG View APIs for programmatic data retrieval and integration
- Supports compound similarity search, substructure search, and property-based filtering

## Use Cases in Research

- Retrieving structural and physicochemical properties of antimicrobial compounds for QSAR modeling
- Mining bioactivity data for known antibiotics and identifying structure-activity relationships
- Linking drug candidates to known targets and resistance mechanisms in pathogens
- Building chemical training datasets for AI-driven antibiotic discovery pipelines
- Cross-referencing chemical scaffolds across pharmacological datasets for repurposing studies

## Getting Started

Visit [PubChem](https://pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
